project.name: OrbitCart
project.description: Checkout service with a traceable pricing incident

events.title: Launch the first pricing flow
events.summary: Added a minimal checkout calculation based on product prices.
events.recorded_reason: OrbitCart needed a reliable baseline before promotions and payment processing.
recorded_risks: Pricing rules are intentionally simple and do not yet support discounts.

events.title: Support promotional codes at checkout
events.summary: Added percentage discount codes and allowed checkout to apply a list of codes.
events.recorded_reason: Marketing needed launch promotions for new customers.
recorded_risks: Multiple codes can currently be combined without a policy check.

events.title: Document duplicate discount incident OC-17
events.summary: Recorded a production report showing the same promotion being applied twice.
events.recorded_reason: The original discount loop trusted the incoming list and had no uniqueness rule.
recorded_risks: Orders can be undercharged when clients resend a code.

events.title: Reject duplicate promotion applications
events.summary: Deduplicated promotion codes before calculating the order total.
events.recorded_reason: This closes OC-17 with a narrow validation guard.
recorded_risks: Promotion policy remains embedded in checkout and may become difficult to extend.

events.title: Capture timezone expiry failure OC-31
events.summary: Documented promotion expiry differences between server time and customer time.
events.recorded_reason: Date handling was added informally as campaigns became more complex.
recorded_risks: Valid promotions can be rejected around midnight.

events.title: Centralize totals and promotion rules
events.summary: Moved discount calculation into a single pricing module and made time an explicit input.
events.recorded_reason: Fixing OC-31 safely required one consistent location for pricing decisions.
recorded_risks: The pricing module is now a critical dependency for every checkout.

events.title: Record repeated pricing latency OC-44
events.summary: Captured evidence that repeated pricing calculations slowed large carts.
events.recorded_reason: Centralization made correctness clearer but placed more work on the checkout path.
recorded_risks: Checkout abandonment may increase if latency continues to rise.

events.title: Cache product prices during checkout
events.summary: Added an in-memory price cache to avoid repeated product lookups.
events.recorded_reason: This directly addresses the latency reported in OC-44.
recorded_risks: Cached prices have no invalidation path when catalog prices change.

events.title: Report stale checkout prices OC-52
events.summary: Recorded that checkout can retain an old price after a catalog update.
events.recorded_reason: The new cache stores prices indefinitely and never observes catalog changes.
recorded_risks: Customers may be overcharged or undercharged.

events.title: Disable unsafe price cache
events.summary: Removed cache reads from pricing while preserving the module for follow-up work.
events.recorded_reason: Correct totals are more important than the latency improvement.
recorded_risks: OC-44 performance symptoms may return until a safe design ships.

events.title: Key prices by catalog version
events.summary: Reintroduced caching with catalog versions and an explicit invalidation function.
events.recorded_reason: Versioned keys preserve the OC-44 speedup without retaining obsolete prices.
recorded_risks: Catalog callers must increment the version whenever a product price changes.

events.title: Prevent stale price regression
events.summary: Added a regression test proving a new catalog version cannot reuse an old cached price.
events.recorded_reason: OC-52 needs executable evidence that the corrected behavior remains safe.
recorded_risks: Cache performance under production load still requires monitoring.
